thought his doctors were trying to kill him. 
YATES said he 
was afraid he was losing his mind. 
These bottles of medicine 
had been prescribed for him by several doctors over an extended 
period of time. 
It was suggested to YATES that he contact his 
own doctor, 
or seek help at Parkland Hospital in Dallas, Texas, 
the City-County Hospital.

"In my statement of December 10, 1963 I mentioned: 
‘After 
I got back from Irving, 
that same day, 
I told DEMPSEY 
JONES, 
a man that 
I work with, about the conversation 
I had 
ae 
with this man.' 
I wish to explain that my conversation with 
DEMPSEY was as follows: 
I mentioned to DEMPSEY that a co-incidence 
had happened that morning, explaining that I had picked up a 
hitch-hiker who said the same thing that DEMPSEY and 
I had 
talked about previously, namely, that a man could be shot 
from a window or from a building top when a man would be coming 
— 
up from under the Continental Underpass. 
This is all 
I can 
3 
remember about my conversation with DEMPSEY. 
Outside of the above changes my statement of December 10, 
1963 is accurate, 
I have read this statement and it is true an_ correct,
